note: Mathlib/Topology/Sion.lean:444 -- Sion.minimax proves the Sion-von Neumann minimax theorem for complete linear orders; file docstring explicitly notes 'As a particular case, one get[s] the von Neumann theorem' and a TODO to 'Spell out the particular case of von Neumann theorem' is still open, so the classical (von Neumann) minimax theorem the QID denotes is a special case not yet spelled out -- generalization, not exact.
verify_note: Decl confirmed in cache+source; note's line 444 actually points to Sion.minimax' (the correct Sion.minimax is at line 336, docstring 'A minimax theorem without completeness'), but the semantic claim re-verified directly against the file's TODO section ('Spell out the particular case of von Neumann theorem') holds -- Wikidata Q913849 is the generic minimax-theorem concept, Sion's result is a genuine strict generalization of it, so 'generalization' is honest and not an overclaim.

note: Mathlib/NumberTheory/ArithmeticFunction/Misc.lean:143 -- `def sigma (k : ℕ) : ArithmeticFunction ℕ := ⟨fun n => ∑ d ∈ divisors n, d ^ k, ...⟩`, docstring 'σ k n is the sum of the kth powers of the divisors of n' -- this IS the divisor function (sigma function).
verify_note: Decl and docstring confirmed verbatim at line 143. Wikidata Q915474 sitelinks to en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Divisor_function, whose subject IS the generalized sigma_k function (k=0 gives count, k=1 gives sum) -- exact match is correct, not overclaimed.

note: Mathlib/NumberTheory/NumberField/ClassNumber.lean:64 -- `noncomputable def classNumber : ℕ := Fintype.card (ClassGroup (𝓞 K))`, docstring 'the class number of a number field is the (finite) cardinality of the class group of its ring of integers' -- the standard definition of class number.
verify_note: Decl and docstring confirmed verbatim at line 64. Wikidata Q91783763 description is literally 'order of an ideal class group', sitelinked to en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Class_number_(number_theory) -- exact textual match to the decl's definition, not overclaimed.

note: Field-level container: Mathlib/FieldTheory/Galois/ (Basic.lean, Infinite.lean, Abelian.lean, NormalBasis.lean, GaloisClosure.lean, Profinite.lean, IsGaloisGroup.lean, Notation.lean) is the dedicated top-level home for Galois theory in Mathlib (~2853 decls under Mathlib.FieldTheory total, verified sub-key 'Galois' present in catalog/data/hierarchy.json libraries.Mathlib.modules.FieldTheory.sub). Galois theory is a whole field/discipline, not a single object -- container, not a decl join.
verify_note: Traversed catalog/data/hierarchy.json myself: libraries.Mathlib.modules.FieldTheory.sub.Galois exists with n_decls=210 and exactly the 8 sub-files (Basic/Infinite/Abelian/NormalBasis/GaloisClosure/Profinite/IsGaloisGroup/Notation) the note lists. Wikidata Q92552 = 'Galois theory', instance-of 'field of study' -- genuinely a discipline whose formal Mathlib home is this folder, not an overclaimed single-decl join.

note: Mathlib/Topology/Bases.lean:695 -- `class _root_.FirstCountableTopology : Prop where nhds_generated_countable : ...`, docstring 'A topology in which 𝓝 x is countably generated for every x' -- this IS the first-countable space definition.
verify_note: Decl confirmed at line 695 with docstring 'A first-countable space is one in which every point has a countable neighborhood basis.' Wikidata Q926996 description is 'topological space in which every point admits a countable neighborhood basis' -- near-verbatim match, exact is correct.
